question.label: Context sources
question.name: When you need context before making a code decision, where do you go first?
question.description: Shows whether he knows the right people and channels for architecture context.

answer: I am trying not to interrupt people, so sometimes I keep questions in a notes doc instead of asking.

assessment.note: He is withholding questions to avoid interrupting people, which strongly signals unclear onboarding channels and a mild growth drag from not seeking context directly.

new_queue.label: Notes doc habit
new_queue.name: That notes doc habit — what makes a question feel interruptive to ask?

new_queue.label: Support network
new_queue.name: Who should be part of your go-to support circle here?

new_queue.label: Next support
new_queue.name: What would make the next two weeks easier for you?

issues: coverage: injected wellbeing into next question (0 touches after turn 4)

history.answer_excerpt: I am comfortable reading the services, but I am less sure how decisions are really made here.
I can trace most request paths now, though I still miss historical context behind certain tradeoffs.
I have not been fully clear on who is on point for each domain when priorities conflict.
I am trying not to interrupt people, so sometimes I keep questions in a notes doc instead of asking.
